core_vaccines.name: Feline Panleukopenia Virus (FPV)
core_vaccines.description: Protects against feline panleukopenia (feline distemper), a highly contagious and often fatal viral disease.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: Starting at 6 weeks of age, every 3-4 weeks until 16-20 weeks of age (minimum 2 doses)

core_vaccines.booster: Single dose 1 year after last initial dose, then every 3 years

core_vaccines.name: Feline Herpesvirus Type 1 (FHV-1)
core_vaccines.description: Protects against feline viral rhinotracheitis, a major cause of upper respiratory infection in cats.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: Starting at 6 weeks of age, every 3-4 weeks until 16-20 weeks of age (minimum 2 doses)

core_vaccines.booster: Single dose 1 year after last initial dose, then every 3 years

core_vaccines.name: Feline Calicivirus (FCV)
core_vaccines.description: Protects against feline calicivirus, a common cause of upper respiratory infections and oral disease in cats.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: Starting at 6 weeks of age, every 3-4 weeks until 16-20 weeks of age (minimum 2 doses)

core_vaccines.booster: Single dose 1 year after last initial dose, then every 3 years

core_vaccines.name: Rabies
core_vaccines.description: Protects against rabies virus. Required by law in most jurisdictions. Fatal zoonotic disease.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: Single dose at 12-16 weeks of age as required by law
initial_series.cats_over_16_weeks: Single dose as required by law

core_vaccines.booster: As required by law — typically 1 year after initial, then every 1-3 years depending on product and jurisdiction
core_vaccines.route: Subcutaneous (non-adjuvanted preferred) — right hind limb distal to stifle

noncore_vaccines.name: Feline Leukemia Virus (FeLV)
noncore_vaccines.description: Protects against feline leukemia virus. Recommended for all kittens and cats with outdoor access or exposure to FeLV-positive cats.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: 2 doses, 3-4 weeks apart, starting at 8 weeks of age

noncore_vaccines.booster: Single dose 1 year after last initial dose. Subsequent boosters based on risk — annually for high-risk cats, may discontinue for indoor-only adults
noncore_vaccines.route: Subcutaneous — left hind limb distal to stifle

noncore_vaccines.name: Feline Immunodeficiency Virus (FIV)
noncore_vaccines.description: Protects against feline immunodeficiency virus. May be considered for cats at high risk of exposure through fighting.
noncore_vaccines.booster: Annually for cats with continued risk

noncore_vaccines.name: Chlamydia felis
noncore_vaccines.description: Protects against chlamydial conjunctivitis. Recommended for multi-cat environments with confirmed chlamydia infections.

initial_series.kittens_under_16_weeks: 2 doses, 3-4 weeks apart, starting at 9 weeks of age

noncore_vaccines.booster: Annually for cats with continued risk

noncore_vaccines.name: Bordetella bronchiseptica (Feline)
noncore_vaccines.description: Protects against bordetella respiratory infection. May be considered for cats in high-density environments.

initial_series.all_ages: Single intranasal dose at 8 weeks of age or older

noncore_vaccines.booster: Annually for cats with continued risk
